label: Real
review_text: Bought this iPhone 15 Pro for work travel after my old XS started lagging. Battery comfortably lasts a full day with heavy use including video calls and maps. The titanium frame feels lighter in hand but I noticed the back gets warm during 4K recording. Camera is excellent in daylight, mediocre in low light compared to my colleague's Pixel 8. Worth the upgrade if you're coming from older models, skip if you have a 14 Pro.

label: Real
review_text: Used this blender for 3 months making smoothies and protein shakes daily. Crushes ice well but struggles with frozen fruit chunks unless I add liquid first. The pulse button is finicky — have to press hard. Cleaning is easy if you rinse immediately. Not worth Vitamix money but solid for the price. Replaced my old Nutribullet which leaked after 8 months.

label: Real
review_text: Honestly disappointed. The hinges feel cheap and one already squeaks after 2 weeks. Assembly took 4 hours because the holes weren't aligned on two of the panels. Customer service replaced one panel after I sent photos but I had to pay return shipping. The wood is okay quality once assembled but for the price I expected much better. Would not buy again.

label: Real
review_text: Got these running shoes for marathon training. Logged 200 miles so far. Great cushioning but the heel collar started rubbing after mile 15 — had to use Body Glide. Sizing runs slightly small, ordered half size up which was perfect. Outsole shows minimal wear. Would buy again but in a wide width next time.

label: Real
review_text: I had high hopes for this coffee maker but the temperature control is unreliable. Sometimes brews at 195F as advertised, other times barely hits 180F which leaves coffee tasting weak. Reached out to support, they sent a replacement which has the same issue. The thermal carafe is excellent though, keeps coffee hot for 4+ hours. Mixed feelings.
